definition: The state or fact of having a duty to deal with something or of having control over someone.
example: With great power comes great responsibility.

definition: The ability or capacity to do something or act in a particular way; political or social authority or control.
example: With great power comes great responsibility.

definition: A load, typically a heavy one; a duty or misfortune that causes hardship.
example: Power is a burden and a gift most people will never understand—and with that power comes responsibility.

definition: An act of selecting or making a decision when faced with two or more possibilities.
example: It is our choices, Harry, that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.
source: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets (2002)

definition: The events that will necessarily happen to a particular person or thing in the future.
example: Destiny still leaves you a choice: all we have to decide is what to do with the time that is given us.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001)

definition: A moral or legal obligation; a responsibility.
example: Duty is simple to say and hard to live: with great power comes great responsibility—don’t ever forget that.

definition: An act of giving up something valued for the sake of something else regarded as more important or worthy.
example: Sacrifice puts the needs of the many above the needs of the few, or the one.
source: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan (1982)

definition: The quality of being honest and having strong moral principles; moral uprightness.
example: Integrity is not who I am underneath, but what I do that defines me.

definition: Great bravery; actions that are courageous and noble, often in service of others.
example: Heroism can be a quiet stand: I am no man—and still I will not yield.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King (2003)

definition: The fact or condition of being accountable; responsibility for one's actions.
example: Accountability refuses the sideline: I can’t just watch bad things happen; I have to do something; I have to be better.

definition: The guidance provided by a mentor, especially an experienced person in a company or educational institution.
example: Mentorship names the gift and the weight: you have a power most will never understand, and with it responsibility.

definition: A result or effect of an action or condition.
example: Consequence follows power: if you can do good for others, you have a moral obligation to do those things.

definition: With no special features; normal—until action reveals more.
example: Ordinary people become extraordinary when they refuse to look away.

definition: To take up a position of resistance or support.
example: I can’t just stand by—accountability begins when watching becomes participating.

definition: A firmly held belief or opinion.
example: Conviction without evidence is noise; conviction after struggle is leadership.

definition: The quality of being coherent and intelligible.
example: Clarity is kindness in speech—confusion is often a way to hide.
